paper_title: Space Vector Modulation for Induction Motor on ARM-based Microcontroller

entities.id: College of Engineering Technology, Can Tho University
entities.description: Institution where the research was conducted, located in Viet Nam.
The primary field of study involving motor drives and power electronics.
A pulse width modulation technique for motor drive systems.
The specific 32-bit hardware platform used for implementation.
A proposed method based on coaxial gear system and fixed-point arithmetic for frequency precision.
Pulse Width Modulation technique used to obtain three-phase voltages.
Arithmetic approach used to optimize computations on processors without floating-point units.
The specific Intelligent Power Module (IPM) model used in the experiment.
The application domain for the implemented modulation technique.
A type of power converter that converts DC to AC.
The resolution of the output frequency in a control system.
The minimum achievable output frequency without significant error.
The computational power required for the SVM implementation at 5 kHz.
The rate at which SVM calculations are performed.
The achieved frequency resolution of the proposed rotation method.
The measured error in output frequency during simulation.
The resolution of the look-up table used for trigonometric functions.
The operating frequency of the Cortex M3 CPU.
The practical maximum output voltage with an inverter module.
